journal_entry.description: Discounting of 90-day promissory note at bank (collateralized borrowing)
journal_entry.rationale:
Management has determined this is a collateralized borrowing, so the note receivable remains on the books. The transaction records: (1) Cash received from the bank (net proceeds after discount), (2) A liability to the bank for the borrowed amount, and (3) Interest expense for the discount charges. 

Calculation: The note has 40 days remaining (90 - 50). Discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16.

Under IFRS, when risks and rewards are not transferred, the note receivable stays as an asset, cash increases by the net proceeds, a liability is recognized for the amount owed to the bank (the face value of $100,000), and the discount is expensed immediately as a financing cost.

The debit tuple (1,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one asset increase (Cash) and one expense increase (Interest/Discount Expense). The credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability increase (Loan Payable or Notes Payable - Bank). This matches the collateralized borrowing treatment.

output.reason: This is a collateralized borrowing (not a sale of the receivable). On the debit side: (1) Cash = asset increase (receiving net proceeds from bank), and (2) Interest expense = expense increase (the discount charge represents interest cost on the borrowing). The note receivable remains on Ford's books since risks and rewards were not transferred. Two distinct debit lines: one for the cash received and one for the financing cost incurred.

debit_classifier.reason: This is a collateralized borrowing (not a sale of the receivable). On the debit side: (1) Cash = asset increase (receiving net proceeds from bank), and (2) Interest expense = expense increase (the discount charge represents interest cost on the borrowing). The note receivable remains on Ford's books since risks and rewards were not transferred. Two distinct debit lines: one for the cash received and one for the financing cost incurred.

output.reason: The transaction involves discounting a note receivable at the bank, treated as a collateralized borrowing under IFRS. On the credit side: (1) The note receivable remains on Ford's books (no asset decrease) because risks and rewards are not transferred - this is a borrowing, not a sale. (2) Ford receives cash from the bank, which represents a liability increase (loan payable/note payable to bank). The discount charges will be recorded separately as interest expense over time, but the credit side of THIS transaction records only the liability created by the borrowing arrangement. Therefore: 1 line for liability increase (loan from bank).

credit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves discounting a note receivable at the bank, treated as a collateralized borrowing under IFRS. On the credit side: (1) The note receivable remains on Ford's books (no asset decrease) because risks and rewards are not transferred - this is a borrowing, not a sale. (2) Ford receives cash from the bank, which represents a liability increase (loan payable/note payable to bank). The discount charges will be recorded separately as interest expense over time, but the credit side of THIS transaction records only the liability created by the borrowing arrangement. Therefore: 1 line for liability increase (loan from bank).
